betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Particle Chamber: Beam Dump§f

§aBeam Dumps safely absorb an incoming particle beam and convert it to fluids.§f
The §bBeam Dump§f is a square-hollow particle chamber multiblock that accepts an incoming particle beam on one face and outputs §bLiquids§f (the byproducts of particle absorption) from its fluid ports. Use it to dispose of unwanted beams, capture neutron/alpha byproducts, or harvest fluids for downstream processing.

§6Multiblock Structure (BeamDumpLogic.java:62)§f:
- §bExterior§f: any size NxNxM (LengthX = LengthZ, square footprint), §cLengthX must be odd§f (3, 5, 7, …).
- §bCenter block§f: a §bParticle Chamber§f (target) tile at the geometric middle.
- §bBeam ports§f: one or more §bParticle Chamber Beam Ports§f on the outer faces, each connected inward to the center by a line of §bParticle Chamber Beam Blocks§f.
- §bAll beam ports must be INPUT§f — Beam Dumps never output beams (`BeamDumpLogic.java:129`).
- §bAt least 1 ACTIVE fluid output port§f required (set the §bACTIVE§f state on a §bTileParticleChamberFluidPort§f to enable fluid output).
- §bAt least 1§f §bParticle Chamber Energy Port§f (supplies RF for chamber operation).

§6Controller Recipe§f:
The §bBeam Dump Controller§f (`qmd:beam_dump_controller`) is crafted in a vanilla §bminecraft.crafting§f grid from:
- §bElite Plating§f (`nuclearcraft:part:3`)
- §bTough Alloy Ingot§f (`nuclearcraft:alloy:1`)
- §bBasic Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:4`)
- §bCopper Block§f (`libvulpes:metal0:4` or Chisel variants)

§6Beam Dump Recipes (BeamDumpRecipeMaker / BeamDumpCategory)§f:
Beam Dump recipes (in `lach_01298.qmd.recipes.BeamDumpRecipes`) map particle beams to fluid outputs. For example, an antiproton beam may be dumped to produce hydrogen or exotic fluids depending on the recipe. Recipes are not exposed in JEI by default; consult the QMD wiki or in-game documentation for the current recipe list.

§cWarning§f: A Beam Dump that has a beam port set to OUTPUT, has no ACTIVE fluid output port, or is not square/odd-sized will fail the `isMachineWhole()` validation and refuse to form.

§eTip§f: Use the Beam Dump as the §bend-of-line§f for any LINAC experiment — it absorbs the beam safely instead of letting particles dissipate in mid-air, and gives you back fluids you can route to an §bNC Decay Hastener§f or §bNC Fluid Irradiator§f for further processing.
